title: VK2809 VOYAGE Study: Viking Therapeutics Reports Positive Phase 2b Results for NASH/MASH
text: Viking Therapeutics announced final results from its Phase 2b VOYAGE study of VK2809, a liver-selective thyroid hormone receptor beta agonist, presented at the 75th Liver Meeting 2024. Patients receiving VK2809 achieved mean relative liver fat reductions of 37-55% at Week 52, with 64-88% experiencing 30% or greater reduction compared to placebo. The drug demonstrated impressive results on secondary endpoints with NASH resolution in 63-75% of VK2809-treated patients versus 29% for placebo, and fibrosis improvement achieved in 44-57% with no NASH worsening. Combined endpoint results showed 40-50% achieved both NASH resolution and fibrosis improvement. Treatment produced placebo-adjusted LDL cholesterol reductions of 20-25%, plus improvements in triglycerides and atherogenic proteins including lipoprotein(a) and apolipoprotein B. The majority (94%) of treatment related adverse events among patients receiving VK2809 were reported as mild or moderate. Discontinuation rates were low and balanced with placebo, and gastrointestinal tolerability remained excellent throughout the 52-week study. Viking plans to advance VK2809 toward potential regulatory submission based on these results, while continuing development of VK2735 for obesity and other metabolic programs.
